# Internationalization (i18n)

Webspresso provides built-in i18n support with JSON-based translations and automatic locale detection.

## Translation Files

Translations are organized hierarchically:

- **Global**: `pages/locales/{locale}.json`
- **Route-specific**: `pages/{route}/locales/{locale}.json`

Route-specific translations override global ones.

## Global Translations

Create translation files in `pages/locales/`:

```json
// pages/locales/en.json
{
  "nav": {
    "home": "Home",
    "about": "About",
    "contact": "Contact"
  },
  "app": {
    "name": "My App",
    "description": "A great app"
  }
}
```

```json
// pages/locales/tr.json
{
  "nav": {
    "home": "Ana Sayfa",
    "about": "Hakkında",
    "contact": "İletişim"
  },
  "app": {
    "name": "Uygulamam",
    "description": "Harika bir uygulama"
  }
}
```

## Route-Specific Translations

Override translations for specific routes:

```json
// pages/about/locales/en.json
{
  "title": "About Us",
  "description": "Learn more about our company"
}
```

```json
// pages/about/locales/tr.json
{
  "title": "Hakkımızda",
  "description": "Şirketimiz hakkında daha fazla bilgi edinin"
}
```

## Using Translations

### In Templates

Use the `t()` function:

```njk
{# pages/index.njk #}
<h1>{{ t('app.name') }}</h1>
<nav>
  <a href="/">{{ t('nav.home') }}</a>
  <a href="/about">{{ t('nav.about') }}</a>
</nav>
```

### In Route Configs

Access translations via context:

```javascript
// pages/about/index.js
module.exports = {
  meta(req, ctx) {
    return {
      title: ctx.t('title'), // Uses route-specific translation
      description: ctx.t('description'),
    };
  },
};
```

## Locale Detection

Webspresso automatically detects the locale from:

1. URL parameter: `?locale=en`
2. Cookie: `locale=en`
3. Accept-Language header
4. Default locale (from `DEFAULT_LOCALE` env var)

## Supported Locales

Configure supported locales:

```bash
# .env
DEFAULT_LOCALE=en
SUPPORTED_LOCALES=en,tr,fr
```

## Programmatic Access

Access translations in route configs:

```javascript
// pages/blog/index.js
module.exports = {
  async load(req, ctx) {
    // ctx.t is the translation function
    const title = ctx.t('blog.title');
    
    return {
      title,
      posts: await fetchPosts(),
    };
  },
};
```

## Nested Keys

Use dot notation for nested keys:

```json
{
  "errors": {
    "validation": {
      "required": "This field is required",
      "email": "Invalid email address"
    }
  }
}
```

```njk
{{ t('errors.validation.required') }}
{{ t('errors.validation.email') }}
```

## Fallback

If a translation key is not found:

1. Route-specific translations are checked first
2. Global translations are checked
3. The key itself is returned as fallback

## Examples

### Navigation

```njk
{# views/partials/nav.njk #}
<nav>
  <a href="/">{{ t('nav.home') }}</a>
  <a href="/about">{{ t('nav.about') }}</a>
  <a href="/blog">{{ t('nav.blog') }}</a>
  <a href="/contact">{{ t('nav.contact') }}</a>
</nav>
```

### Error Messages

```json
// pages/locales/en.json
{
  "errors": {
    "404": "Page not found",
    "500": "Server error"
  }
}
```

```njk
<h1>{{ t('errors.404') }}</h1>
```

### Form Labels

```json
// pages/contact/locales/en.json
{
  "form": {
    "name": "Name",
    "email": "Email",
    "message": "Message",
    "submit": "Send"
  }
}
```

```njk
<form>
  <label>{{ t('form.name') }}</label>
  <input type="text" name="name">
  
  <label>{{ t('form.email') }}</label>
  <input type="email" name="email">
  
  <button>{{ t('form.submit') }}</button>
</form>
```
